text: The mapping-and-inventory repo is the central Librarian Hub of the Citadel Mesh. It runs a dual-process HuggingFace Space: Streamlit UI on port 7860 and a FastAPI sidecar on port 8000. The FastAPI sidecar exposes: GET /healthz (liveness), GET /v1/stats (RAG index info), POST /v1/ingest (rebuild FAISS index), GET /v1/query and POST /v1/query (semantic search), POST /v1/system/commit (GitHub file commit bridge, requires X-HF-Token), GET /v1/system/tunnels (HuggingFace+GDrive+GitHub reachability), GET /v1/system/status (aggregate health + device file counts + uptime), POST /v1/ingest/device (device-specific reindex), POST /v1/webhook/github (HMAC-validated push webhook), POST /v1/bridge/ingest (universal spoke RAG ingest), GET /v1/bridge/spokes (spoke registry), GET /v1/telemetry (full Vercel HUD diagnostic dump).

The RAG engine (services/rag_hub.py) uses sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2 (384-dim) to embed text chunks into a FAISS flat-L2 index stored in data/vector_store/harvest.index. Chunks are 1200 chars with 150-char overlap. DEFAULT_FRAGMENT_GLOBS covers TIA_MASTER_HARVEST.txt, AETHER_HARVEST_COMPLETE.md, harvest_report.md, manifests, plus all DEVICE_FRAGMENT_GLOBS. DEVICE_FRAGMENT_GLOBS covers S10_CITADEL_OMEGA_INTEL, Partition_01-04+46, Research/S10, master_intelligence_map.txt. Index persists across container restarts. FAISS and sentence-transformers are imported lazily.

Two physical device nodes feed intelligence via Termux git pushes. S10 Field Uplink (Mackay, QLD): pushes to S10_CITADEL_OMEGA_INTEL/ and Research/S10/; syncs to gdrive:CITADEL_OMEGA_INTEL/ via rclone; workflow: s10_push_to_vault.yml. Oppo Librarian Node (Finch Hatton, QLD): pushes directly to Partition_01/ via Termux git. Both nodes trigger POST /v1/webhook/github on push, auto-queuing a FAISS reindex of device paths without requiring a container rebuild.

The Universal Bridge (services/universal_bridge.py) accepts RAG payloads from any spoke at POST /v1/bridge/ingest. Headers are normalised case-insensitively and hyphen/underscore agnostically: X-Spoke-Name, x_spoke_name, and X-Node-Name are all accepted. Auth: SPOKE_SHARED_SECRET validated with hmac.compare_digest. Fragments array is written to data/spoke_artifacts/<spoke>/rag_fragments.txt and triggers background FAISS reindex. Artifacts dict writes individual files. Registry updated in data/spoke_sync_registry.json on every call. Also: GET /v1/bridge/spokes (spoke registry) and POST /v1/bridge/heartbeat (liveness ping).

The Self-Healing Monitor (core/self_healing_logic.py) HEAD-probes every registered spoke HuggingFace Space (8s timeout). If a Space returns 503, 502, 500, or 404: logs to data/monitoring/self_healing_log.json; pushes a restart commit to the spoke GitHub repo via the Contents API to trigger HF rebuild; if HTTP 404 and deploy/skeleton/ exists, pushes full skeleton (app.py + requirements.txt + README.md). Requires GH_TOKEN with contents:write. Daemon mode: --daemon --interval 300. Spoke list loaded from data/handshake_manifest.json.

The Key Rotator (key_rotator_module.py) discovers all API keys for any env-var family: PREFIX, PREFIX_2, PREFIX_3, PREFIXS (comma-separated bundle). Returns a thread-safe round-robin KeyRotator. call_with_rotation(fn, rotator) calls fn(key) and on rate-limit errors (429/quota/ResourceExhausted) automatically advances to the next key. Works for GEMINI_API_KEY, HF_TOKEN, or any other env-var prefix.

The repo has 65 GitHub Actions workflows. Key ones: s10_push_to_vault.yml (sync S10 intel to GDrive), hf_space_sync.yml (notify HF Spaces on push), spoke_sync_receiver.yml (receive repository_dispatch from spokes), mesh_heartbeat.yml (30-min health check), repair_all_spaces.yml (fix broken HF Spaces), forever_learning_orchestrator.yml (continuous learning), master_harvester.yml (data harvest), tia_citadel_deep_scan.yml (deep scan). Race condition risk: mesh_heartbeat.yml and worker_watchdog.yml both commit worker_status.json concurrently. Fix: add concurrency groups.

VAMGUARD (documentation/directory names: vamguard_templates/, VAMGUARD_TITAN/) and VANGUARD (Python code: Partition_01/vanguard_v*.py) refer to the same system. VAMGUARD = branding name. VANGUARD = code variable/class name. Do NOT rename either. Treat as semantic aliases. VAMGUARD_TITAN contains the wheel-and-spokes infrastructure for FLEET-WATCHER, CIPHER-NEXUS, and AETHER-NEXUS spokes.

The 321GB GENESIS_VAULT on Google Drive is the primary data substrate. Access via rclone with RCLONE_CONFIG_DATA (base64 rclone.conf blob) or GDRIVE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON. Key GDrive paths: gdrive:CITADEL_OMEGA_INTEL/ (S10 intel), gdrive:GENESIS_VAULT/S10_CARGO/ (S10 cargo), gdrive:GENESIS_VAULT/OPPO_CARGO/ (Oppo cargo). Data is harvested into GitHub via gdrive_partition_harvester.yml, gdrive_worker_harvester.yml, and gdrive_model_ingester.yml workflows.

Required Hub environment variables: HF_TOKEN (CRITICAL), GH_TOKEN/GITHUB_TOKEN (CRITICAL), GEMINI_API_KEY (CRITICAL), GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET (HIGH), SPOKE_SHARED_SECRET (HIGH), GDRIVE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON (HIGH), RCLONE_CONFIG_DATA (HIGH), GEMINI_API_KEY_2/_3 (MEDIUM), HF_WEBHOOK_MAPPING (MEDIUM). Check token presence (never values) via GET /v1/system/status -> tokens object or GET /v1/telemetry -> active_keys object.

The Vacuum Shard Worker classifies data/ files into four Specialist Domains: TECH (code, scripts, models, datasets, configs, agents, pipelines), BIO (spiritual, genetics, health, frequency, personal archive, aetheric logs, lore, personas), LEGAL (legal docs, compliance, PvC ledger, contracts, security), TRADE (trading strategies, crypto tokens, market data, ISO20022, blockchain). Files copied to data/shards/<DOMAIN>/. Manifest at data/shards/shard_manifest.json. Run: python workers/vacuum_shard_worker.py

The Oppo device (Finch Hatton, QLD) records 144-Grid signal resonance probes in Partition_01/aetheric_log.txt. Each entry: timestamp, probe description, result file, frequency. Example: [2026-03-30 13:21:28] #GRID | Frequency: 144.12Hz | Location: Finch Hatton North | Status: RESONANT | Source: OPPO_FORGE. These logs are RAG-indexed via DEVICE_FRAGMENT_GLOBS Partition_01/*.txt pattern.

scripts/deploy_fixer.sh pushes a minimal Streamlit skeleton (app.py + requirements.txt + README.md) to all 11 spoke repos to clear HuggingFace No Application File errors. Usage: GH_TOKEN=<pat> bash scripts/deploy_fixer.sh [--dry-run] [--force] [--repo REPO1 REPO2]. Target repos: ARK_CORE, TIA-ARCHITECT-CORE, CITADEL_OMEGA, ORACLE, AION, VAMGUARD_TITAN, Genesis-Research-Rack, Pioneer-Trader, Citadel_Genetics, goanna_coding, mapping-and-inventory. Requires curl, jq, base64, GH_TOKEN.

GET /v1/telemetry returns the full Vercel Admiral HUD diagnostic payload: device_sync (Oppo/S10 file counts and last push timestamps), rag_health (FAISS loaded/chunks/sources), active_keys (count of Gemini/HF/GH keys configured — never values), error_logs (last 20 errors from self-healing log and workers), spoke_registry (per-spoke last-seen and sync count), shard_summary (domain shard file counts). Also returns uptime_s and version.

data/handshake_manifest.json is the canonical mapping of every Citadel spoke to its GitHub URL, HF Space URL, required secrets, push paths, and integration status. Spokes listed: ARK-CORE (integrated), TIA-ARCHITECT-CORE (repair-needed), VAMGUARD-TITAN, CITADEL-OMEGA, ORACLE, AION, GENESIS-RESEARCH-RACK, S10-FIELD-UPLINK (active), OPPO-LIBRARIAN (active), PIONEER-TRADER, CITADEL-GENETICS, GOANNA-CODING. Hub connection protocol: POST /v1/bridge/ingest with X-Spoke-Token header or POST /v1/webhook/github with HMAC-SHA256.
